rationale: The "satisfying drop-and-resolve" emotional pitch maps well to Marble Sort's calm/satisfying tone and casual female 25-55 audience, so audience overlap is strong. However, there are real frictions: the archetype's reds/pinks/browns and expressive-faced fruit characters clash with Marble Sort's pastel lavender palette and characterless, rounded 3D style, and Suika-style merging is a fundamentally different mechanic from color-sorting via conveyor — using this hook risks bait-and-switch and IPM/retention drop. Only worth testing if reshot with the actual sorting loop styled to mimic Suika's "drop & resolve" satisfaction beats, not as a literal fruit-merge clone.
